language.label: Language
language.hint: Restart may be needed for the native window menu to switch language.

topbar.accountTooltip: Account — local-only identity. Click to rename or set up sync.

tree.emptyNoProject: Select or create a project to see collections.
tree.emptyNoCollections: No collections yet. Click + to add one, or import from OpenAPI/Postman.

settings.title: Settings
settings.userAgentHint: Sent on every request unless the request defines its own User-Agent header.
settings.userAgentBrowserWarning: Browser mode can't set User-Agent. Run as desktop (Tauri) for this to actually go out on the wire.
settings.localDataHint: Collections, requests, environments, variables and scripts live in a SQLite database in the application data directory and survive app restarts. Small UI state (sync token, collapse state, settings) lives in the webview's local storage. Backups are plain JSON and round-trip both.
settings.webviewStorageTooltip: Whether the OS/browser has marked the webview's storage (localStorage / IndexedDB) as evict-resistant. Only affects small UI state — the SQLite file is always persistent.
settings.requestPersistenceTooltip: Ask the OS to mark webview storage as evict-resistant. Already attempted at app start; click to retry if it didn't take.
settings.webviewUsedTooltip: Webview-managed cache only (does not include the SQLite file).
settings.importConfirmMessage: Import will REPLACE every organization, collection, request, environment, variable and script in your local storage with what is in this backup. This cannot be undone. Continue?
settings.resetLocalDataHint: Wipe every organization, project, collection, request, environment, variable and script on this device. Useful when switching servers or accounts and the leftover local state confuses sign-in. Sync tokens and settings are also cleared. Export a backup first if you might need this data again.
settings.resetLocalDataConfirmMessage: Deletes every organization, project, collection, request, environment, variable, and script on this device. Sync tokens, settings, language, and UI state are cleared. The schema stays in place. This cannot be undone.
settings.syncOptional: Optional. Pushes local data to an Aelvory server so you can pick up on another device.
settings.serverUrlHint: e.g. https://aelvory.example.com or http://192.168.1.100:5000. Leave empty to use the built-in default. Changing the URL while signed in will sign you out — different server means different account.
settings.serverUrlInvalid: Must be a valid http(s) URL, or empty for the built-in default.
settings.serverUrlChangeMessage: Switching from "{from}" to "{to}" will sign you out of sync. Local data stays. Continue?
settings.signOutMessage: Local data stays. You can sign back in anytime; sync will resume where it left off (server keeps your rows).
settings.e2eeHint: When on, the server stores only ciphertext. Your passphrase never leaves this device. Merge/conflict handling is limited (server can't inspect encrypted rows) — leave off if you plan to sync often across devices.
settings.passphraseHint: Re-enter each session. If you forget this passphrase, your server-side data stays encrypted and unreadable — export a backup before enabling if you want an escape hatch.
settings.needsPassphrase: E2EE is enabled but the passphrase isn't unlocked — sync is paused.

about.title: About Aelvory
about.ossIntro: Aelvory is built on top of these open-source projects (and many more transitively). Thanks to their authors and maintainers.
about.disclaimerBody: Aelvory is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind. The authors and contributors take no responsibility for any data loss, security incident, downtime, or other damages arising from its use. You are responsible for verifying that requests, scripts, and imports do what you expect against your own systems before running them.

toast.noProjectForCollection: Select a project first, then create a collection in it.
toast.noProjectForEnvironment: Select a project first, then create an environment.
toast.syncConflictsDetail: Another device had newer changes for {types}{more}. Your version was discarded.
toast.settingsServerUrlUnchangedDetail: Effective URL is the same as before.
toast.settingsServerUrlSwitchedDetail: Signed out of the old server. Sign in to the new one to resume sync.
toast.signedInDetail: Sync is enabled. Click "Sync now" to push your local data.
toast.e2eeUnlockedDetail: Key derived — you can now sync encrypted data.
